message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker and input-types improvements

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl tracker state. Input-types lib.rs additions
for click interval and input event handling improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-ioyl2","title":"[audit][T10.1] Complete asupersync select migration in runtime_compat.rs","description":"# Complete asupersync select migration in runtime_compat.rs

## Problem
crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs line 899:
  // TODO(asupersync): replace with asupersync-native select when available
  // and tokio channel dependencies have been fully migrated.

Currently both the asupersync-runtime and non-asupersync-runtime feature paths re-export tokio::select. The asupersync path should use asupersync-native select instead, but this CANNOT be done until all tokio channel types (mpsc, watch, oneshot) used in select! arms are migrated to asupersync equivalents.

## Prerequisites (documented in code comments lines 890-900)
1. All tokio::sync::mpsc channels used in select! arms must be migrated to asupersync channels
2. All tokio::sync::watch channels must be migrated
3. asupersync must expose a stable select! macro

## Relationship to existing work
This is part of the ft-e34d9.10 epic (asupersync migration). Specifically:
- ft-e34d9.10.2: Core runtime substrate convergence (includes channel migration)
- ft-e34d9.10.3: Structured concurrency and cancellation correctness

## Acceptance Criteria
1. When asupersync-runtime feature is enabled, runtime_compat::select uses asupersync-native select
2. TODO comment removed
3. All tests pass under both feature configurations
4. No tokio channel types remain in select! arms when asupersync-runtime is active","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:52.082733Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:57.552281Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","audit","runtime","todo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ioyl2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:23.903906Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2501,"issue_id":"ft-ioyl2","author":"SagePlateau","text":"Cross-epic dependency: this bead depends on ft-e34d9.10.2 (Core runtime substrate convergence) from the asupersync migration epic ft-e34d9.10.

The dependency is correctly wired. Do NOT attempt this migration until the channel types are migrated — the code comments at runtime_compat.rs:890-900 explain why in detail. The two changes (channels + select) must be coordinated or select! arms will have type mismatches.

Current code (both feature paths re-export tokio::select):
  #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
  pub use tokio::select;
  #[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]
  pub use tokio::select;

Target state:
  #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
  pub use asupersync::select;
  #[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]
  pub use tokio::select;","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-iqf","title":"E2E integration checklist: comprehensive coverage validation by feature","description":"# E2E Integration Checklist: Comprehensive coverage validation

## Purpose
Ensure every major feature has **end-to-end coverage** with:
- deterministic synchronization (no fixed sleeps)
- detailed, structured logging
- artifact bundles that make failures diagnosable

This checklist is **human-friendly**.
The **canonical, enforceable** source of truth for “what runs under `--all`” is the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`).

## How to use this checklist
For each item below:
- ensure there is a corresponding E2E case in the registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`)
- ensure the case runs via the standard runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`)
- ensure the case follows the harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`)

If an item is intentionally *not* E2E-tested (e.g., requires human credentials), document the reason and ensure we at least have:
- fixture-based integration tests, and/or
- a manual smoke-test command with stable output and artifact capture

## Checklist by feature area

### Phase 1: Core features

#### Ingest (`wa-4vx.4`)
- [ ] E2E: basic text capture from a real WezTerm dummy pane
- [ ] E2E: delta extraction produces correct segments (no duplication)
- [ ] E2E: FTS indexing enables search
- [ ] Artifacts: segment stats (bytes/lines), ingest lag, FTS timing

#### Pattern detection (`wa-4vx.5`)
- [ ] E2E: known patterns trigger events (at least 1 per critical workflow trigger)
- [ ] E2E: false positives are rejected (near-miss negative fixture)
- [ ] E2E: extraction captures expected structured facts
- [ ] Artifacts: rule_id matched, extracted facts JSON, match timing

#### Daemon/runtime (`wa-4vx.6`)
- [ ] E2E: `wa watch` starts, runs, and exits cleanly
- [ ] E2E: graceful shutdown flushes storage queue and releases lock
- [ ] E2E: restart recovers cleanly (no corrupt state)
- [ ] Artifacts: health snapshot (queues/lag), watcher logs, lock state

#### Robot mode (`wa-4vx.7`)
- [ ] E2E: core robot commands produce valid JSON envelopes
- [ ] E2E: stable error codes for common failures (pane missing, policy denied)
- [ ] E2E: `wa robot send` verification (`--wait-for`) works (or `PaneWaiter` equivalent)
- [ ] Artifacts: raw JSON outputs for each command + schema validation results

#### Safety/policy (`wa-4vx.8`)
- [ ] E2E: policy denies blocked actions (alt-screen, recent gap, prompt not active)
- [ ] E2E: approval allow-once flow works and is audited
- [ ] E2E: audit trail captures allow/deny decisions with redaction
- [ ] Artifacts: audit export slice, redaction proof scans (no secrets)

### Phase 2: Workflows

#### Workflow engine (`wa-nu4.1.1`)
- [ ] E2E: workflow triggered by an event under `--auto-handle`
- [ ] E2E: workflow step logging is complete and ordered
- [ ] E2E: workflow resumes after restart (idempotent; no duplicate sends)
- [ ] Artifacts: workflow execution logs + step log export

#### Usage limits (`wa-nu4.1.3`)
- [ ] E2E: end-to-end usage-limit workflow **fixture-first** (no real auth)
- [ ] E2E: key failure modes are safe and actionable (MFA required, cannot pick account)
- [ ] Artifacts: parsed resume/session info (redacted), next-step plan output

#### Compaction (`wa-nu4.1.2`)
- [ ] E2E: compaction detected and handled exactly once per event (dedupe/cooldown)
- [ ] E2E: injected context is verified (echo/marker)
- [ ] Artifacts: detection evidence, injection payload preview (redacted), verification tail hash

### Phase 3–4: Polish / integration

#### Diagnostics/health/metrics (`wa-nu4.3.4`)
- [ ] E2E: `wa doctor` healthy vs broken output is actionable
- [ ] E2E: metrics endpoint (Prometheus) responds when enabled (`wa-nu4.3.4.5`)
- [ ] E2E: watcher health snapshot is visible via CLI (`wa-nu4.3.4.2`)
- [ ] Artifacts: diag bundle layout + redaction proofs (`wa-nu4.3.4.7`, `wa-nu4.3.4.8`)

#### Notifications (`wa-psm`)
- [ ] E2E: events trigger notifications (webhook mock server)
- [ ] E2E: throttling prevents spam
- [ ] E2E: failure recovery works (network down, endpoint 500)
- [ ] Artifacts: delivery attempts, retry/backoff, persisted notification history

#### CLI polish (`wa-rnf`)
- [ ] E2E: shell completion generation works (at least smoke)
- [ ] E2E: alias expansion is correct
- [ ] E2E: help text stays accurate (docs-smoke style)

#### Timeline/correlation (`wa-6sk`)
- [ ] E2E: events appear in timeline
- [ ] E2E: correlations are deterministic on fixtures
- [ ] E2E: query performance acceptable under a seeded dataset

#### Quick-fix suggestions (`wa-bnm`)
- [ ] E2E: suggestions appear on common errors/events
- [ ] E2E: suggestions are copy-pasteable and safe
- [ ] Artifacts: suggestion IDs fired + dismissal persistence (if applicable)

## E2E case requirements (non-negotiable)
- No fixed `sleep N` synchronization; use wait-for conditions with timeouts.
- All cases write an artifacts directory and print its path on failure.
- Every case prints a PASS/FAIL summary with elapsed time and key assertions.

## Registry
- Not a case. This checklist validates that registry coverage matches feature coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every checklist item maps to a registry case (or is explicitly justified as non-E2E)
- [ ] All cases follow `wa-4vx.10.6` contract and run under `wa-4vx.10.11`
- [ ] CI runs `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all --verbose` and uploads artifacts on failure (`wa-nu4.3.9.6`)

## Testing
- Meta: add a lightweight “checklist-to-registry” validator that fails if this checklist references removed epics/tasks or if required areas are missing from the registry.
- Ensure the validator itself is deterministic and runs in CI.

-{"id":"ft-ism9v","title":"[audit][T9.1] Implement Hyper and Meta keyboard modifier handling","description":"# Implement Hyper and Meta keyboard modifier handling

## Problem
frankenterm/input-types/src/lib.rs lines 1760 and 1903:
  // TODO: Hyper and Meta are not handled yet.
  Modifiers::SUPER, /* TODO: Hyper and Meta should be added here. */

The keyboard input encoding does not handle Hyper and Meta modifiers. These are used by power users (especially Emacs users) and some tiling WM configurations. Key events with these modifiers are silently dropped or misinterpreted.

## Fix
1. Add Hyper and Meta to the modifier encoding at line 1903
2. Implement modifier decoding at line 1760
3. Map to appropriate CSI/SS3 sequences per the kitty keyboard protocol spec

## Context
The kitty keyboard protocol (which ft supports) defines encoding for Hyper and Meta. The data structures already have Modifiers::HYPER and Modifiers::META — only the encoding/decoding is missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Hyper and Meta modifiers are correctly encoded in keyboard reports
2. Decoded from incoming key events
3. Unit tests for each modifier and combinations
4. Both TODO comments removed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:22.374217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:53.382051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","input","keyboard","vendored"]}
+{"id":"ft-ism9v","title":"[audit][T9.1] Implement Hyper and Meta keyboard modifier handling","description":"# Implement Hyper and Meta keyboard modifier handling

## Problem
frankenterm/input-types/src/lib.rs lines 1760 and 1903:
  // TODO: Hyper and Meta are not handled yet.
  Modifiers::SUPER, /* TODO: Hyper and Meta should be added here. */

The keyboard input encoding does not handle Hyper and Meta modifiers. These are used by power users (especially Emacs users) and some tiling WM configurations. Key events with these modifiers are silently dropped or misinterpreted.

## Fix
1. Add Hyper and Meta to the modifier encoding at line 1903
2. Implement modifier decoding at line 1760
3. Map to appropriate CSI/SS3 sequences per the kitty keyboard protocol spec

## Context
The kitty keyboard protocol (which ft supports) defines encoding for Hyper and Meta. The data structures already have Modifiers::HYPER and Modifiers::META — only the encoding/decoding is missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Hyper and Meta modifiers are correctly encoded in keyboard reports
2. Decoded from incoming key events
3. Unit tests for each modifier and combinations
4. Both TODO comments removed","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:22.374217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T22:58:45.148785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","input","keyboard","vendored"]}
 {"id":"ft-itft","title":"Notification backend interface + redaction pipeline","description":"## What
Define a unified notification interface and centralized redaction before dispatch.

## Why
Desktop notifications and webhooks exist today but use separate code paths; a shared interface prevents drift and ensures consistent safety.

## How
- Define a NotificationSender trait used by desktop + webhook + future channels
- Centralize payload formatting + redaction (before any network call)
- Wrap with rate-limit + cooldown using existing NotificationGate

## Risks
- Must preserve current behavior and config compatibility

## Success Criteria
- Existing desktop/webhook paths refactored to the shared interface
- Redaction applied uniformly across channels","notes":"Implemented notification pipeline (NotificationGate + redacted payload fanout), added webhook transport in CLI, and normalized stored event severity/agent_type casing. Added pipeline unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:59.443810636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.274333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T06:06:00.206449742Z","close_reason":"Implemented notification interface + redaction pipeline","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-itft","depends_on_id":"ft-j1ke","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ivkrv","title":"[audit][T6.2] Migrate all three handler constant blocks to CassQueryConfig","description":"# Migrate all three handler constant blocks to CassQueryConfig

## What
Replace the three constant blocks in workflows/handlers.rs:
1. Lines 1015-1020: SESSION_START_CASS_* -> CassQueryConfig::session_start()
2. Lines 1572-1576: ON_ERROR_CASS_* -> CassQueryConfig::on_error()
3. Lines 2301-2305: AUTH_CASS_* -> CassQueryConfig::auth()
4. Line 2068: SWARM_LEARNING_INDEX_TIMEOUT_SECS -> tuning.workflows.swarm_learning_index_timeout_secs
5. Line 2836: CLAUDE_CODE_LIMITS_COOLDOWN_MS -> tuning.workflows.claude_code_limits_cooldown_ms

For each handler, replace direct constant references with config struct field access. The handler receives its CassQueryConfig from TuningConfig.workflows at initialization.

## Verify
After migration, grep for the old constant names — they should not appear anywhere in the codebase except in test fixtures or comments documenting the migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero references to old constant names in production code
2. All three handlers use CassQueryConfig
3. All existing tests pass (behavior identical)
4. New tests verify each handler uses its specific config values","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:25.771643Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:34:24.865467Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:34:24.865219Z","close_reason":"All 3 handler constant blocks (SESSION_START, ON_ERROR, AUTH) + standalone cooldowns migrated to CassQueryConfig/WorkflowsTuning refs. SagePlateau.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","config","workflows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ivkrv","depends_on_id":"ft-fled9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:42.325325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ivkrv","depends_on_id":"ft-ulhwv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:43.378045Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ixt4","title":"Create WezTerm fork with wa feature flag and minimal integration points","description":"## Overview

Fork WezTerm and add the minimal code needed to support wa event delivery. All changes are behind \`#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]\`.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: wa-wyr1 (WaEventSink trait design)

## Repository Setup

### 1. Fork WezTerm
\`\`\`bash
gh repo fork wez/wezterm --clone
cd wezterm
git checkout -b feature/wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Add Feature Flag
In Cargo.toml (workspace root):
\`\`\`toml
[workspace.features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

In relevant crates (mux/Cargo.toml, wezterm/Cargo.toml):
\`\`\`toml
[features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

## Modification Points

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
use wa_events::WaEventSink;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
static WA_EVENT_SINK: OnceCell<Arc<dyn WaEventSink>> = OnceCell::new();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {
    if let Some(sink) = WA_EVENT_SINK.get() {
        event(sink.as_ref());
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:
\`\`\`rust
fn process_pty_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
    // Existing WezTerm code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_output(self.pane_id, data);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit State Changes

In methods that update pane state:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_title(&mut self, title: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    self.emit_state_change();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn emit_state_change(&self) {
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_state_change(self.pane_id, &self.get_wa_state());
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 4. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit User-Var Changes

In the OSC 1337 handler:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_user_var(&mut self, name: String, value: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_user_var_changed(self.pane_id, &name, &value);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 5. wezterm/src/main.rs — Initialize Sink

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn init_wa_integration() {
    if let Ok(socket_path) = std::env::var("WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET") {
        match wa_socket_sink::connect(&socket_path) {
            Ok(sink) => mux::register_wa_event_sink(sink),
            Err(e) => log::warn!("Failed to connect to wa socket: {}", e),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 1. Build with Feature
\`\`\`bash
cargo build --features wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Default Build Unaffected
\`\`\`bash
cargo build  # Should work identically to upstream
\`\`\`

### 3. Integration Test
\`\`\`bash
# Start wa watch (which creates the socket)
wa watch &

# Start WezTerm with wa integration
WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET=/tmp/wa/events.sock ./target/debug/wezterm

# Verify events received
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WezTerm fork created with feature/wa-integration branch
- [ ] Feature flag added to workspace
- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths
- [ ] Event emission added to user-var handler
- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs
- [ ] Events actually received by wa (integration test)

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

## Files to Modify (in WezTerm fork)

- Cargo.toml (workspace) — add feature
- mux/Cargo.toml — add feature
- mux/src/lib.rs — event sink registration
- mux/src/pane.rs — event emission
- wezterm/Cargo.toml — add feature
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialization
- NEW: mux/src/wa_events.rs — trait + socket sink implementation

## References

- WezTerm mux: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Rust feature flags: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.103113431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.308598Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.653011Z","close_reason":"Superseded by wa-2umk2: FrankenTerm in-tree integration replaces minimal fork strategy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ixt4","depends_on_id":"ft-bjm9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ixt4","depends_on_id":"ft-wyr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-m76j","title":"FTUI-03.2 Enforce one-writer output routing for UI and log surfaces","description":"## Background\nfrankentui requires a one-writer rule. wa must eliminate direct stdout/stderr writes from active UI runtime paths.\n\n## Deliverables\n- unified output sink routing UI redraw + logs\n- explicit policy for subprocess output capture/routing\n- violation detection strategy in tests/CI\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- no direct terminal writes bypass output gate in migrated paths\n- sustained output does not corrupt cursor/layout state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:51.521424086Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.287279Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:44:58.628915122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-m76j","depends_on_id":"ft-f5wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-m79p","title":"Implement OpenAI/Codex device auth flow (auth.openai.com/codex/device)","description":"# Task: OpenAI device auth flow (Playwright)

## Goal
Automate the OpenAI device-code flow used by Codex CLI via Playwright in a way that is:
- robust to small UI changes
- safe with respect to secrets (no password storage)
- debuggable when it fails (screenshots/HTML dumps, redacted logs)

Target URL:
- `https://auth.openai.com/codex/device`

## Preconditions / inputs
- A persistent Playwright profile exists for the target account (profile dir derived from workspace/config).
- The workflow step has already obtained a `user_code` (device code) from the Codex pane.

## Happy path (v0)
1. Navigate to the device page.
2. Ensure we are in a logged-in state for the intended account:
   - if already logged in: proceed
   - if email prompt appears: fill email, continue
   - if password/MFA required: **do not attempt to automate**; exit into the fallback path (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
3. Fill the `user_code` form field.
4. Submit.
5. Verify success via a stable marker:
   - preferred: page contains “Successfully logged in” (or equivalent stable selector)
   - also capture URL + final DOM snapshot for debugging

## Failure modes
- Password/MFA prompts:
  - return a structured error that the workflow can treat as “interactive bootstrap required”
  - point to the fallback task (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
- Rate limit / bot detection / unexpected page:
  - fail with actionable error and attach redacted artifacts

## Logging / artifacts
- Never log passwords, session cookies, access tokens, or raw HTML that may contain secrets.
- On failure, write artifacts under the workspace artifacts dir:
  - screenshot
  - redacted DOM snapshot (or a hash + minimal selector debug)
  - a short, human-readable failure report

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - parse + validate the `user_code` format before browser automation (fail fast)
- Integration tests (offline):
  - run Playwright against a local HTML fixture that simulates the device page, verifying selector strategy
- Manual smoke tests:
  - a `wa browser smoke-test --openai-device` (tracked elsewhere) confirms it can complete on a real profile

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a valid persistent profile that is already authenticated, this flow completes and returns “success” for a real account.
- If password/MFA is required, the flow exits safely with a clear “interactive bootstrap required” error and produces redacted artifacts.

 {"id":"ft-mhcd","title":"Implement Playwright auth flow for Anthropic (Claude Code /login)","description":"# Task: Implement Playwright auth flow for Anthropic (Claude Code `/login`)

## Goal
Provide an Anthropic login helper that can bring an account into an authenticated state using Playwright + persistent profiles.

Proposed API shape (adjust only if the browser spec bead requires it):
- `async fn complete_anthropic_auth(account: &AccountRecord, ctx: &mut BrowserAutomationContext) -> AuthOutcome`

Where `AuthOutcome` is one of:
- `Success`
- `NeedsHuman { redacted_url, instructions }`
- `Failed { reason, diagnostics }`

## Expected workflow integration
`handle_usage_limits` and `handle_auth_required` for `AgentType::ClaudeCode` will:
1. send `/login` to the pane
2. parse any printed URL/code if present
3. call this browser step
4. wait for pane to become idle / ready

## Implementation outline
### Happy path (already authenticated)
- Create/load persistent profile for `(service=anthropic, account_key)`.
- Navigate to a stable landing page (likely `https://claude.ai/` or console).
- Detect an "already logged in" selector; if present, return `Success`.

### Login path (no session)
- Navigate to the correct login page (start with `https://claude.ai/login` unless `/login` prints a more specific URL).
- If prompted for email, fill using account metadata.
- Continue until either:
  - we reach the authenticated state, OR
  - we detect password/MFA/SSO interstitial

### Password/MFA/SSO handling
- **Do not** ask for, store, or type passwords.
- If a password/MFA step appears, return `NeedsHuman` with:
  - page URL to complete manually (redacted)
  - explicit next-step instructions
  - reminder that after completion, the profile will persist and future runs can be automated

## Safety requirements
- Redact any URLs containing tokens or codes in logs.
- Cap total runtime with a top-level timeout.
- Always close contexts/pages on both success and failure (no zombie browsers).

## Testing
- Integration tests (offline):
  - run Playwright against local HTML fixtures representing:
    - already-authenticated state
    - needs-human state (password/MFA)
  - validate structured outcomes and redaction

- Manual smoke tests:
  - validate on a real machine with a pre-authenticated profile

## Deliverables
- Working Anthropic auth flow using Playwright persistent profiles.
- Clear `NeedsHuman` messaging that can be surfaced by `wa robot workflow ...`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a machine with a pre-authenticated profile, the flow returns `Success` without manual steps.
- On a machine without a profile, the flow either authenticates fully OR returns `NeedsHuman` without leaking secrets.

-{"id":"ft-miqv3","title":"[audit][T11.2] Add integration tests verifying constants are loaded from config","description":"# Add integration tests verifying constants are loaded from config

## What
Integration tests that:
1. Create a temp ft.toml with non-default [tuning] values
2. Initialize ObservationRuntime from that config
3. Verify the runtime and subsystems actually use the configured values (not the old hard-coded defaults)

Example test:
```rust
#[test]
fn runtime_uses_configured_backpressure_threshold() {
    let config = r#"
    [tuning.backpressure]
    warn_ratio = 0.5
    "#;
    // ... write to temp file, load config, verify runtime health check uses 0.5 not 0.75
}
```

## Why
Unit tests verify the struct; integration tests verify the plumbing. A field could have the right default but never actually be READ by the code that needs it.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. At least one integration test per track (T2-T7) verifying end-to-end config loading
2. Tests use non-default values to prove the config path works
3. Tests clean up temp config files","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:24.908060Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:59.776394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","config","integration-tests","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-miqv3","depends_on_id":"ft-fled9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:34.371942Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-ml1b","title":"Agent skills/docs: how to use wa via robot+MCP (swarm playbooks)","description":"# Task: Agent skills/docs (swarm playbooks)

## Goal
Write self-contained guidance for agent swarms to use wa effectively.

## Topics
- When to use `wa robot` vs MCP.
- Canonical flows:
  - check state
  - read text tail
  - search history
  - handle unhandled events
  - run workflows
- Safety expectations:
  - policy gates
  - avoid alt-screen
  - verify after sending

## Deliverables
- `skills/` docs (or equivalent) that can be copied into agent system prompts.

## Validation / tests
Agent playbooks must not drift.

- Prefer to keep examples aligned with:
  - robot JSON schemas (field names)
  - E2E scripts (canonical flows)

- If feasible, add a small CI check that:
  - runs a couple “playbook” robot commands against fixtures
  - asserts outputs are schema-parseable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new agent can operate wa without human coaching.


## Testing
- Drift prevention:
  - Add a CI check (or unit test) that validates embedded robot JSON snippets against schemas.
  - Ensure docs examples are generated/updated from fixtures where possible to avoid bit-rot.
